loki migrate (autonomy/loki:14518, autonomy/migration-agents.sh) already delivers much of the playbook's 'language migration' use case: multi-agent understand/guardrail/migrate/verify phases, Mermaid architecture + data-flow diagram generation (migration-agents.sh:57-63), per-step token-cost + risk estimation, --compliance presets (healthcare/fintech/government), parallel agents, incremental old/new paths, and migration_docs/ output

Loki maps the playbook's phased timeline nearly 1:1 with its 5 healing phases (archaeology/stabilize/isolate/modernize/validate) and even documents the AWS-Transform agent-role mapping and the GitHub 3-agent extract/test/rewrite pattern in skills/healing.md

Loki has an honest scale-constraints table (RepoMod-Bench: 91% pass <10K LOC dropping to 15% >50K LOC) that the playbook omits -- Loki is more truthful about where autonomous modernization breaks down
per_source_findings.loki_current_state: loki heal (autonomy/loki:14110) runs a SINGLE-SHOT provider invocation per phase with a static prompt (autonomy/loki:14429-14454) -- it does NOT go through the RARV run_autonomous verification loop that loki start/migrate use. Env vars LOKI_HEAL_MODE/PHASE/STRICT/PRESERVE_FRICTION and CLI flags (--phase, --resume, --status, --report, --friction-map, --archaeology-only, --strict, --compliance, --parallel, --dry-run) all exist and are wired. Phase gates and friction/characterization enforcement are real (autonomy/hooks/migration-hooks.sh, 745 lines). CRITICAL GAP verified: the behavioral-baseline/ directory is created via mkdir (autonomy/loki:14323) but NOTHING in the codebase captures golden-master outputs into it or compares modernized-vs-legacy outputs against it (grep for writes to behavioral-baseline returns zero non-mkdir hits). The playbook's central accuracy mechanism -- parallel run / side-by-side execution proving IDENTICAL outputs (playbook pp.12,24) -- exists only as prompt instructions in the validate phase, not as an enforced deterministic harness. Characterization verification in heal runs the WHOLE test suite via detect_test_command (migration-hooks.sh:580), not per-system-boundary golden masters, despite references/legacy-healing-patterns.md prescribing system-boundary golden-master capture. No ROI/business-case artifact and no maturity-model self-assessment exist anywhere in heal or migrate (grep confirms ROI/maturity only appear in unrelated memory-system.md/agents.md docs). loki heal does not generate architecture/dependency diagrams (that capability lives only in loki migrate). heal and migrate are two separate, partially-overlapping commands with no unified intelligent routing.

gaps.gap: No deterministic parallel-run / golden-master output-equivalence harness. The behavioral-baseline/ dir is created but never populated or compared. The playbook's core accuracy claim (weeks 3-4 side-by-side identical outputs; weeks 5-8 parallel run comparing outputs) is Loki's biggest trust differentiator yet is only a prompt instruction in the validate phase, not machine-verified.
gaps.loki_worse_because: autonomy/loki:14323 mkdir behavioral-baseline but grep for any write/compare to behavioral-baseline across autonomy/ returns zero non-mkdir/non-config hits; validate-phase 'compare with baseline' is only text in heal_prompt (autonomy/loki:14449, 14423)

gaps.gap: loki heal is single-shot per phase, bypassing the RARV-C verification loop, completion council, and 8 quality gates that make loki start/migrate trustworthy. A healing 'modernize' step gets no iterative reason->act->reflect->verify closure, no override-council on a bad transform, no findings injection.
gaps.loki_worse_because: autonomy/loki:14465-14502 invokes claude/codex/cline/aider once with -p "$heal_prompt"; no call to run.sh/run_autonomous; RARV-C flags in skills/healing.md:266 are documented as 'useful' but not auto-enabled by cmd_heal

gaps.gap: Characterization verification runs the entire test suite (detect_test_command) rather than implementation-agnostic system-boundary golden masters, contradicting Loki's own prescribed approach and the playbook's edge-case-from-production-data coverage. This permits test-overfitting the exact failure RepoMod-Bench warns about.
gaps.loki_worse_because: autonomy/hooks/migration-hooks.sh:580 test_cmd=$(detect_test_command ...); eval whole suite; references/legacy-healing-patterns.md:156-181 itself says system-boundary testing (not unit/full-suite) is correct, so implementation lags its own doc

gaps.gap: No ROI / business-case / cost-of-inaction output. The playbook makes ROI the executive buy-in vehicle (chapter 4) and the Optimized maturity level generates 'modernization proposals with ROI analysis'. Loki produces a health-score but never a business-facing before/after cost, risk-reduction, or maintenance-time-saved artifact.
gaps.loki_worse_because: grep ROI|business case across autonomy/skills/references shows hits only in memory-system.md/agents.md (unrelated); cmd_heal --report (autonomy/loki:14283) emits only friction/failure/knowledge/test counts, no ROI

gaps.gap: No modernization-readiness / maturity self-assessment. The playbook's 4-level maturity model (Ad hoc->Planned->Systematic->Optimized) and the 40%-maintenance-time readiness signal are concrete adoption on-ramps that reduce 'where do I start' friction. Loki has no equivalent triage that tells a user whether/where to modernize.
gaps.loki_worse_because: no maturity/readiness scoring exists in cmd_heal or cmd_migrate; grep maturity returns zero engine hits; health_score (skills/healing.md:342) measures in-progress healing, not pre-flight readiness/target selection

gaps.gap: heal and migrate are two overlapping commands with no unified intelligent routing; diagram generation exists only in migrate, RARV+council only in migrate/start, friction-gates only in heal. A user modernizing legacy code must know which command and which flags to pick -- violating Loki's own 'unified intelligent defaults, never fragmented knobs' principle.
gaps.loki_worse_because: cmd_heal (autonomy/loki:14110) and cmd_migrate (autonomy/loki:14518) are separate dispatch entries with divergent capabilities; migration-agents.sh:57-63 generates Mermaid diagrams but cmd_heal never does; no signal-based auto-selection between them

improvement_candidates.title: Golden-master parallel-run equivalence harness for heal/migrate (the accuracy moat)
improvement_candidates.what: Add a deterministic capture+compare harness that, during archaeology/guardrail, records outputs at natural system boundaries (CLI stdout/exit, HTTP responses, DB state deltas, file/queue outputs) into .loki/healing/behavioral-baseline/ as golden masters, and during modernize/validate re-runs the SAME boundary probes against the modernized code and diffs them. Any divergence blocks the phase gate unless explicitly documented as intentional. This turns the playbook's 'side-by-side identical outputs' (pp.12,24) from a prompt suggestion into machine-verified evidence, and directly feeds Loki's verified-completion/trust-as-product positioning. loki-mode CONSUMES this: it is the same verified-completion evidence gate applied to modernization builds.
acceptance_criteria: cmd_heal archaeology (and migrate guardrail) writes >=1 golden-master artifact per detected boundary into .loki/healing/behavioral-baseline/
hook_healing_phase_gate for modernize->validate fails closed unless boundary outputs match baseline OR a documented-intentional-change record exists
diff report enumerates every changed boundary with old vs new output; emitted in loki heal --report and as structured evidence JSON
works with zero config on a sample C/COBOL and a Python2 fixture repo; degrades honestly ('no boundaries detected') rather than false-green

improvement_candidates.title: Route loki heal modernize/validate through the RARV-C loop + completion council
improvement_candidates.what: When heal reaches stabilize/isolate/modernize/validate (behavior-changing phases), execute via run_autonomous with LOKI_INJECT_FINDINGS/LOKI_OVERRIDE_COUNCIL/LOKI_AUTO_LEARNINGS auto-enabled and the legacy-healing-auditor in the review pool, instead of the current single-shot -p invocation. Archaeology-only can stay single-shot. This gives modernization builds the same iterative verification, override-council, and 8-gate trust that loki start already provides, closing the largest verification gap.
acceptance_criteria: cmd_heal for phases stabilize|isolate|modernize|validate invokes the RARV loop (run.sh path) not a bare claude -p call
RARV-C closure flags default-on within heal (documented in skills/healing.md, verified by grep of cmd_heal)
legacy-healing-auditor fires and can BLOCK on behavioral change without characterization update
a deliberately-broken transform is caught+reverted by the loop, proven on a fixture

improvement_candidates.title: System-boundary golden characterization instead of whole-suite pass/fail
improvement_candidates.what: Replace the current 'run detect_test_command whole suite' verification in hook_post_healing_modify with per-boundary characterization comparison against the golden masters from candidate #1, so verification is implementation-agnostic and cannot be satisfied by overfit unit tests. Keeps whole-suite as an additional signal, not the sole gate.
acceptance_criteria: hook_post_healing_modify compares boundary outputs to baseline, not only whole-suite exit code
a change that keeps unit tests green but alters a CLI/API boundary output is BLOCKED
matches the approach already prescribed in references/legacy-healing-patterns.md:156-181 (self-consistency)

improvement_candidates.title: Modernization readiness + target-selection triage (pre-flight)
improvement_candidates.what: Add loki heal --assess (or a readiness step auto-run before archaeology) that scans a codebase and outputs: estimated LOC/language mix, technical-debt signals, maintenance-risk indicators, a placement on the 4-level maturity model, and a RANKED list of low-risk high-visibility targets to start with (per the playbook weeks 1-2 guidance). Reduces the top adoption-friction question 'where do I start' to one command, and uses Loki's existing complexity-detection machinery.
acceptance_criteria: single command emits a readiness report with maturity-level placement + ranked candidate targets + rationale
ranking prefers isolated/low-blast-radius modules (matches playbook weeks 1-2)
runs read-only (no modification), zero required flags
output is both human-readable and structured JSON for the dashboard

improvement_candidates.title: ROI / cost-of-inaction business-case artifact in loki heal --report
improvement_candidates.what: Extend the healing report to emit a business-facing before/after: maintenance-time signal, risk-reduction (security warnings resolved, single-points-of-failure removed), coverage gained, and a conservative cost-of-inaction framing. Explicitly label estimates as estimates (no fabricated dollar figures). This is the playbook's executive-buy-in vehicle and lowers B2B/enterprise adoption friction, but is a REPORTING layer -- keep it honest and clearly derived from measured metrics, not invented.
acceptance_criteria: loki heal --report includes a business-case section derived only from measured metrics (friction resolved, warnings delta, coverage, boundaries verified)
every quantified claim traces to a measured input; no fabricated market/valuation numbers
opt-in or clearly-marked-estimate to preserve Loki's no-fabrication rule

improvement_candidates.title: Auto-generate C4/dependency + data-flow diagrams during heal archaeology
improvement_candidates.what: Port the Mermaid diagram generation already in loki migrate (migration-agents.sh:57-63) into heal archaeology so every healing run produces architecture + data-flow diagrams that reveal hidden dependencies -- the exact 'Claude created detailed diagrams that revealed hidden dependencies' behavior in the playbook's C->Java sample scenario (p.11). Pure reuse of existing capability; unifies the two commands.
acceptance_criteria: heal archaeology emits architecture.md + data-flows.md with Mermaid into .loki/healing/
diagrams surface at least dependency edges and critical data flows for a sample legacy repo
reuses migration-agents.sh generation, not a new parallel implementation

per_source_findings.source: Devin (Cognition) async/proactive patterns: Managed Devins (parallel sub-agents in isolated VMs), Scheduled Devins (self-scheduling recurring work with cross-session memory), Auto-Triage (event-driven, prompt-injection-sandboxed), and the productivity ESTIMATOR (LLM estimates human-engineering-hours saved per session; r_log 0.74 on 258 sessions from 126 users; powers a $10M AI Productivity Guarantee). Refs: https://cognition.com/blog/ai-productivity, https://devin.ai/guarantee/, https://cognition.ai/blog/ai-guarantee
what_it_does_well: Per-task VALUE ESTIMATOR: an LLM estimates equivalent human-engineering-hours for every completed task, calibrated against a real dataset of engineer time estimates (r_log 0.74 on held-out sessions). Turns raw agent activity into a business-legible ROI number.

Aggregation-honest framing: Cognition explicitly says individual predictions are imperfect but the model is good enough for aggregated totals -- so ROI is reported at the account/team level where the estimator is trustworthy, not oversold per-task.

$ guarantee built ON TOP of the estimator: if delivered engineering value < what the customer paid, they fund usage credits until it does (up to $10M). The metric becomes a contractual enterprise-sales lever, not just a vanity stat.
Auto-Triage is event-driven and prompt-injection-sandboxed: untrusted event content (issue/PR bodies) cannot escalate into arbitrary agent actions.
Scheduled Devins carry cross-session memory so recurring work compounds context instead of cold-starting each run.
Managed Devins run parallel sub-agents in isolated VMs -- true fan-out isolation, not just cwd separation.
per_source_findings.loki_current_state: VERIFIED against source in /Users/lokesh/git/loki-mode. (1) TIME-SAVED metric EXISTS but is a crude fixed heuristic: `time_saved_hours = round(total_iterations * 15 / 60, 2)` (autonomy/loki:23478), rendered by `loki metrics` as 'Estimated: X hours (based on N iterations x 15min each)' (autonomy/loki:23578-23580, comment at :23477 'Each Loki iteration replaces ~15min of manual work'). It is NOT an LLM estimate of human-engineering-hours, is NOT calibrated against real engineer estimates, has no r_log/validation, is project-level session-report only (not per-build), and emits NO dollar ROI figure and NO guarantee. A separate stub exists at autonomy/loki:13879 (`'estimated_hours': '?'`) in the migration-plan writer -- literally a hardcoded question mark. (2) EVENT-DRIVEN: autonomy/trigger-server.py is a real GitHub webhook receiver with HMAC-SHA256 signature validation, a bounded worker queue (webhook-storm safe), child-process reaping, and a flag-injection defense (REPO_FULL_NAME_RE at :48, blocking dash-leading repo names from becoming `loki start` flags -- the same threat class Devin's prompt-injection-sandboxed triage addresses). BUT on `issues.opened` it runs a FULL `loki start <ref> --pr --detach` build (:255) and on `pull_request.synchronize` a full build (:285) -- there is NO lightweight triage/classify/label/route step; it is all-or-nothing build, not triage. (3) SCHEDULED: autonomy/trigger-schedule.py is a cron-like daemon (schedules.json) wired via cmd_trigger (autonomy/loki:14940); Claude Code side has /loop and /schedule skills. No cross-session-memory-carrying recurring agent is described in the schedule entry format. (4) PARALLEL/ISOLATED: git worktrees (skills/parallel-workflows.md) + Docker sandbox (autonomy/sandbox.sh, seccomp/dropped-caps/resource-limits) provide isolation. (5) PROOF: proof.json + advisory 'loki: verified-completion' check-run (autonomy/lib/proof-check.sh) already produce per-build evidence -- a natural carrier for an hours-saved/ROI field.

gaps.gap: No LLM-based human-engineering-hours estimate per build. Loki's only hours number is total_iterations x 15min, a fixed constant unrelated to the actual work done -- a 2-iteration schema tweak and a 2-iteration full-stack feature both score 30min. Devin's estimator reads the actual task/diff and predicts equivalent human effort, calibrated against real engineer estimates (r_log 0.74).
gaps.loki_worse_because: autonomy/loki:23478 `time_saved_hours = round(total_iterations * 15 / 60, 2)`; comment :23477 states the fixed 15min assumption. No LLM call, no diff/task input, no calibration anywhere in the codebase (grep for calibrat/r_log returns nothing relevant).

gaps.gap: No dollar ROI figure and no per-build ROI. The metric is only in the project-level `loki metrics` session report; there is no per-build 'this build saved ~X engineer-hours (~$Y)' surfaced in proof.json, the dashboard, or PR comments -- exactly the enterprise-sales/adoption lever Devin monetized.
gaps.loki_worse_because: The `time_saved` block (autonomy/loki:23533-23537) has only hours/iterations/minutes_per_iteration, no cost, no per-build scope. proof.json (autonomy/lib/proof-check.sh) surfaces an honesty headline but carries no hours/ROI field.

gaps.gap: Hours-saved is uncalibrated and unvalidated, so it cannot honestly power a guarantee or even a credible marketing claim. Loki's own binding rule is 'accuracy is the moat, no fabrication' -- shipping `iterations x 15min` as 'time saved' is the kind of unbacked number that rule warns against.
gaps.loki_worse_because: No held-out validation set, no engineer-estimate ground truth, no error metric anywhere; the 15min constant is asserted in a code comment (autonomy/loki:23477) with no evidence.

gaps.gap: Event-driven entrypoint jumps straight to a full build with no triage/classify/route stage. A webhook on every opened issue spawns a full autonomous build (cost + risk), where Devin first triages (is this actionable? label/route/estimate) before committing compute. This is both a cost-control and a false-positive-build gap.
gaps.loki_worse_because: autonomy/trigger-server.py:255 handles issues.opened by running full `loki start <ref> --pr --detach`; there is no intermediate classify/triage action -- the only actions are full builds.

gaps.gap: Scheduled recurring runs do not carry forward cross-session memory as a first-class feature. Loki HAS a memory system, but the schedule entry format (trigger-schedule.py:8-16) has no memory-thread/continuity field, so a recurring 'daily-quality-review' cold-starts context each run rather than compounding it like Scheduled Devins.
gaps.loki_worse_because: trigger-schedule.py schedule entry schema (name/cron_expr/action/args/enabled/last_run) has no memory-session or continuity key; nothing binds recurring runs to a persistent episodic thread.

improvement_candidates.title: loki-mode: LLM-calibrated engineering-hours estimator, emitted into proof.json per build
improvement_candidates.what: Replace the `iterations x 15min` heuristic with an LLM estimator that reads the actual completed work (diff stat, files changed, tests written, task/PRD scope, iteration transcript) and predicts equivalent human-engineering-hours, plus a low/high band. Persist it as an `effort_estimate` field in the existing per-build proof.json (autonomy/lib/proof-check.sh) so it rides the trust/evidence rail loki already ships, and fall back to the current heuristic when no LLM is available (labeled 'heuristic' vs 'estimated' so the number is never silently fabricated). Applicable: loki-mode is the engine that produces the diff/proof and already makes provider calls, so it genuinely consumes this. This is the load-bearing primitive both the dollar ROI and any guarantee depend on.
acceptance_criteria: proof.json gains an effort_estimate object: {hours, low, high, method: 'llm'|'heuristic', model, inputs_hash}
`loki metrics` TIME SAVED block reads the per-build estimates when present and only falls back to iterations x 15min when absent, labeling which method produced the number
Estimator input is the real work (diff stat + files + tests + task scope), NOT iteration count alone; verified by a unit test feeding two builds of different scope and asserting different hours
No LLM available -> heuristic path, clearly labeled 'heuristic (uncalibrated)'; never presented as a validated estimate

improvement_candidates.title: loki-mode: per-build ROI surface (hours + $) in dashboard, PR comment, and `loki metrics`
improvement_candidates.what: Once the estimator exists, surface 'this build delivered ~X engineer-hours (~$Y at a configurable blended rate)' in three places: the verified-completion PR comment/check-run, the dashboard build view, and `loki metrics`. $ is hours x an operator-set LOKI_ENGINEER_HOURLY_RATE (default off / unset shows hours only, so we never invent a dollar figure). Applicable: this is loki-mode surfacing its own build output; it is the concrete adoption + enterprise-sales lever, and it stays honest by making $ opt-in and always showing the underlying estimator method/band.
acceptance_criteria: Verified-completion PR comment includes the hours band and (only if rate configured) a $ figure
Dashboard build view shows the same estimate with method label
$ requires LOKI_ENGINEER_HOURLY_RATE; unset -> hours-only, never a fabricated dollar amount
Aggregate account/team view sums per-build hours (matches Devin's aggregation-honest framing rather than trusting any single estimate)

improvement_candidates.title: loki-mode: add a lightweight TRIAGE action to trigger-server before it spawns a full build
improvement_candidates.what: Insert a classify/triage step in autonomy/trigger-server.py between the (already-hardened) webhook auth and the full `loki start`. On issues.opened, run a cheap model pass over the issue body to decide: actionable-build | needs-info (post a comment) | label-and-route | ignore -- and only escalate to a full build on actionable-build. Keep the existing HMAC + REPO_FULL_NAME_RE flag-injection defenses (they already match Devin's prompt-injection-sandboxed posture) and treat the issue body as untrusted throughout. Applicable: loki-mode owns trigger-server.py and the loki start entrypoint; this directly reduces wasted full builds and false-positive PRs, which is the cost/adoption concern.
acceptance_criteria: issues.opened routes through a triage classifier; only 'actionable-build' verdicts call full `loki start`
Non-actionable verdicts take a cheap action (comment/label/skip) and are logged via the existing log_event path
Untrusted issue/PR body text can never alter the triage action set or inject a loki start flag; covered by a test with an adversarial body (dash-leading, injection strings)
Triage is default-conservative: ambiguous -> needs-info comment, not an auto-build

improvement_candidates.title: autonomi-SaaS: hours-saved / ROI receipt and optional productivity guarantee, consuming loki-mode's proof.json effort_estimate
improvement_candidates.what: In autonomi-saas, overlay the per-build effort_estimate from loki's proof.json onto the Evidence Receipt and aggregate it into an account-level ROI dashboard ('this month: ~N engineer-hours, ~$M saved'). Because it is aggregated across builds, it matches the level where the estimator is honest. This is the natural home for a Devin-style guarantee (SaaS carries billing, tenancy, and the commercial relationship); loki-mode should NOT own the guarantee. Applicable: autonomi-saas already overlays stored evidence onto the engine proof (per MEMORY Evidence Receipt work), so it genuinely consumes this field; the guarantee itself is gated on the estimator being calibrated first.
acceptance_criteria: BFF reads proof.json effort_estimate per build and stores it against the tenant/build
Account view aggregates hours (+ $ if rate set) across builds, labeled with the aggregation-honest caveat
Guarantee logic (if pursued) is founder-gated and blocked until the estimator has a validation number (do not ship a $ guarantee on an uncalibrated estimate)
No cross-tenant leakage in the aggregate (respects the per-build two-read split already used for #17)

improvement_candidates.title: loki-mode: calibrate the estimator against a ground-truth engineer-estimate set (validation gate)
improvement_candidates.what: Build a small validation harness: collect a set of completed builds with human engineer time estimates (from real usage / benchmark tasks), score the LLM estimator against them, and report an error metric (e.g. r_log or MAE-in-log-space) checked into the benchmark harness. Until this exists, the estimate must always be labeled 'uncalibrated'. Applicable: this is what lets loki/autonomi make ANY honest hours claim and is the precondition for the guarantee; it directly serves the 'accuracy is the moat, no fabrication' binding rule. Ties into existing task #49 (benchmark harness).
acceptance_criteria: A validation dataset of builds with engineer-hour ground truth exists in the benchmark harness
Estimator error metric is computed and reported; a threshold gates whether estimates may be labeled 'calibrated' vs 'uncalibrated'
Marketing/UI/guarantee copy may only claim validated hours once the metric clears the threshold
Harness is reproducible (fits task #49's reproducible-benchmark goal)
